free_text: Activation cross section data of deuteron induced
nuclear reactions on rubidium up to 50 MeV
Fine powder of high purity (99.8 %) Rb2SO4 (Sigma
Aldrich) sedimented onto 50 um Al foils.
Sedimented layer was covered by 10 um Al foil.
Activation method was used to measure
cross section.
HPGe detector based Gamma-spectrometry was
used to measure the activity.
The stack was irradiated with deuteron beam of
50 MeV, 27 nA for 40 minutes.
Energy degradation was calculated by using the
polynomial approximation of Ziegler.
The irradiation was done on an extracted beam of
the cyclotron.
The activities of the produced radionuclides were
measured nondestructively without chemical separation.
Nuclear decay data were taken from NUDAT-2.8 database.
For estimation of the uncertainty of the
beam energy in the target foils, cumulative effects of
the possible uncertainties were considered:
primary energy uncertainty, target thickness,
effect of the energy straggling,
correction for the monitor reaction.
The uncertainty of the cross sections was
obtained from all the individual contributions:
incident charged particle flux (7. %)
target thickness (6. %)
detector efficiency  (5. %)
nuclear data (3. %)
statistical uncertainty (1.0-20. %)
The experimental results were compared to the
result calculated by the ALICE-IPPE, EMPIRE-II and
TALYS-1.6 theoretical model codes. Results for TALYS
are from TENDL-2017 and TENDL-2019 online library.
Integral yields were calculated from the
fitted experimental cross sections.
ST.
